rawOutput: {"status":"fail","violations":[{"file":"internal/exec/agent.go","line":113,"issue":"Agent execution is hard-coded to launch `claude`, but workflows default `workflow.agent` to `claude-code` and the selected agent is never consulted. On machines that only have `claude-code` installed, every agent step will fail at runtime even though the workflow validated successfully.","fix":"Pass the configured workflow/step agent executable through execution options/context and build the command from that value instead of the string literal. For example, change `buildAgentArgs` to accept an `agentCmd string` and start with `args := []string{agentCmd}`; initialize it from `workflow.Agent` (defaulting once during load) and thread it into `ExecuteAgentStep`.","priority":"high","status":"new"},{"file":"internal/runner/resume.go","line":73,"issue":"Resume restores `SessionIDs` from state, but it never restores the corresponding `LastSessionStepID`. `ResolveResumeSession` and `ResolveInheritSession` depend on that field, so any resumed workflow that uses `session: resume` or `session: inherit` will silently lose its prior conversation and either fail validation or start a fresh session unexpectedly.","fix":"Persist and restore the most recent session key together with `SessionIDs`. A concrete fix is to add `LastSessionStepID` to the serialized state, plumb it through `Options`/`RootContextOptions`, set it when reading state in `ResumeWorkflow`, and initialize `ExecutionContext.LastSessionStepID` from that restored value.","priority":"high","status":"new"},{"file":"cmd/agent-runner/main.go","line":162,"issue":"Top-level CLI errors are now dropped on the floor. `rootCmd.Execute()` failures exit with status 1 without printing the error, so invalid workflow files, validation failures, and runtime errors become silent failures with no user-visible explanation.","fix":"Print the returned error before exiting, e.g. `if err := rootCmd.Execute(); err != nil { fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, \"agent-runner: %v\\n\", err); os.Exit(1) }`.","priority":"medium","status":"new"}]}

violations.issue: Agent execution is hard-coded to launch `claude`, but workflows default `workflow.agent` to `claude-code` and the selected agent is never consulted. On machines that only have `claude-code` installed, every agent step will fail at runtime even though the workflow validated successfully.
violations.fix: Pass the configured workflow/step agent executable through execution options/context and build the command from that value instead of the string literal. For example, change `buildAgentArgs` to accept an `agentCmd string` and start with `args := []string{agentCmd}`; initialize it from `workflow.Agent` (defaulting once during load) and thread it into `ExecuteAgentStep`.
violations.result: buildAgentArgs now accepts agentCmd parameter from ExecutionContext.AgentCmd, which is set from Workflow.Agent during initRunState

violations.issue: Resume restores `SessionIDs` from state, but it never restores the corresponding `LastSessionStepID`. `ResolveResumeSession` and `ResolveInheritSession` depend on that field, so any resumed workflow that uses `session: resume` or `session: inherit` will silently lose its prior conversation and either fail validation or start a fresh session unexpectedly.
violations.fix: Persist and restore the most recent session key together with `SessionIDs`. A concrete fix is to add `LastSessionStepID` to the serialized state, plumb it through `Options`/`RootContextOptions`, set it when reading state in `ResumeWorkflow`, and initialize `ExecutionContext.LastSessionStepID` from that restored value.
violations.result: Added LastSessionStepID to NestedStepState, Options, and restore flow in ResumeWorkflow

violations.issue: Top-level CLI errors are now dropped on the floor. `rootCmd.Execute()` failures exit with status 1 without printing the error, so invalid workflow files, validation failures, and runtime errors become silent failures with no user-visible explanation.
violations.fix: Print the returned error before exiting, e.g. `if err := rootCmd.Execute(); err != nil { fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "agent-runner: %v\n", err); os.Exit(1) }`.
violations.result: Added fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr) before os.Exit(1) to print the error
